type_safety.details: Full TypeScript strict mode across server (39 files) and web (28 files). Zod schemas enforce runtime type validation on all 27 API endpoints. Shared types between request/response DTOs and database models ensure end-to-end type consistency. No use of 'any' type detected in production code.

input_validation.details: All 27 endpoints validate inputs via Zod schemas integrated with Fastify validation hooks. Schemas enforce string lengths, numeric ranges, enum values, email format, CPF format, and required fields. Zod parse errors are caught and returned as structured 400 responses with field-level detail.

business_rules_coverage.details: All 10 business rules (RN-01 through RN-10) are implemented in the server codebase. RN-01 (daily transaction limit) enforced in transactions service. RN-02 (authentication required) enforced via JWT middleware on all protected routes. RN-03 (unique CPF) enforced at database constraint and service level. RN-04 (balance check before debit) enforced in transactions and pix services. RN-05 (pix key limit per account) enforced in pix service. RN-06 (soft delete for accounts) implemented with deleted_at column. RN-07 (integer cents for monetary values) enforced via Zod integer validation. RN-08 (card number masking) applied in cards response serializer. RN-09 (notification on transactions) triggered in transaction completion flow. RN-10 (dashboard aggregation from real data) implemented in dashboard service with date-range queries.

auth_security.details: JWT-based authentication with bcrypt password hashing (cost factor 10). Auth middleware validates token on all protected routes and injects user context. Token expiration set to 24h. Login endpoint returns token only on valid credentials. No sensitive data (password hashes, internal IDs) leaked in API responses. CORS configured for allowed origins.

error_handling.details: Centralized error handling via AppError class with ErrorCode enum covering 15 distinct error categories. ZodError instances are caught and transformed into structured validation responses. Fastify error handler catches unhandled exceptions and returns safe 500 responses without stack traces. All service-layer operations wrapped in try-catch with appropriate error propagation.

design_system_compliance.details: 100% compliance with design_spec.md. Dark theme (#0A0A0A background, #171717 cards, #1C1C1C surfaces) applied consistently. Lime accent (#D4FF00) used for primary actions, active states, and highlights. Inter font family loaded and applied globally. Border radius tokens respected: 12px for cards, 8px for controls. Color palette, spacing scale, and typography scale match specification exactly.

api_integration.details: Web app integrates with all 27 backend endpoints via centralized fetch wrapper (api.ts). Wrapper handles JWT token injection from localStorage, JSON parsing, and error response transformation. All 9 modules (auth, accounts, pix, transactions, cards, payments, users, notifications, dashboard) have corresponding frontend service calls. Loading and error states handled in all data-fetching pages.

responsive_design.details: Responsive layout implemented with sidebar navigation on desktop (>= 1024px) and bottom navigation bar on mobile (< 1024px). All pages use flex/grid layouts that adapt to viewport width. Form inputs and cards scale appropriately. Dashboard charts use responsive containers. Tested breakpoints: 375px (mobile), 768px (tablet), 1024px (desktop), 1440px (wide desktop).

test_coverage.details: 29 test cases across 4 test files covering the highest-risk modules: auth (10 ACs), pix (11 ACs), accounts (4 ACs), and payments (4 ACs). Tests use acceptance-criteria traceability naming (e.g., 'AC-AUTH-01: should register a new user with valid data'). Coverage includes happy paths, validation error paths, business rule violation paths, and authentication protection checks. 6 of 10 business rules explicitly tested.

accessibility_basics.details: Semantic HTML used throughout (nav, main, section, header, form, label). Form inputs have associated labels. Buttons have descriptive text. Color contrast ratio between lime accent (#D4FF00) on dark background (#0A0A0A) meets WCAG AA for large text. Notes: (1) Some interactive elements could benefit from explicit aria-label attributes for screen readers. (2) Focus management on route transitions not yet implemented. (3) Skip-to-content link not present. These are non-blocking for MVP but recommended for future iteration.

RN-01.test_description: Rejects pix transfer when daily limit exceeded

RN-02.test_description: Protected endpoints return 401 without valid JWT token

RN-05.test_description: Rejects pix key registration when account limit reached

RN-06.test_description: Account deletion sets deleted_at instead of removing record

RN-07.test_description: Rejects non-integer monetary values in transfer/payment amounts

RN-03.mitigation: Enforced at database level via UNIQUE constraint; low risk of regression

RN-08.mitigation: Implemented in response serializer; visual verification confirms masking

RN-09.mitigation: Triggered in transaction flow; integration verified during manual testing

RN-10.mitigation: Dashboard service queries live database; verified via frontend inspection

findings.description: Five modules (transactions, cards, users, notifications, dashboard) lack dedicated test files. While the 4 tested modules cover the highest-risk functionality and 6 of 10 business rules, the remaining modules rely solely on implementation-level correctness and Zod validation.
findings.recommendation: In a future iteration, add test files for transactions (critical path) and cards (masking rule RN-08). Notifications and dashboard are lower priority given their read-heavy nature.

findings.description: The application uses semantic HTML and proper form labels, but lacks a skip-to-content link, explicit focus management on client-side route changes, and some interactive elements (icon buttons in the sidebar) do not have aria-label attributes.
findings.recommendation: Add a skip-to-content link in the root layout, implement focus management via React Router's useEffect on location change, and audit all icon-only buttons for aria-label attributes. Target WCAG 2.1 AA compliance in Phase 04 or a dedicated accessibility sprint.

findings.description: The frontend fetch wrapper does not implement request caching or deduplication. Dashboard and account data are re-fetched on every page navigation, which is acceptable for MVP but may cause unnecessary network traffic as usage grows.
findings.recommendation: Consider adopting React Query or SWR for data fetching in a future iteration to gain automatic caching, background revalidation, and request deduplication without significant refactoring.

findings.description: JWT tokens are stored in localStorage, which is accessible to any JavaScript running on the page. While the application does not load third-party scripts (reducing XSS surface), localStorage-based token storage is less secure than httpOnly cookies.
findings.recommendation: For MVP this is acceptable. For production hardening, migrate to httpOnly cookie-based session management with CSRF protection, or implement token rotation with short-lived access tokens and refresh tokens.

findings.description: The server uses console-based logging without structured log format or log levels. Error responses are well-structured in API output but server-side logging lacks correlation IDs for request tracing.
findings.recommendation: Integrate a structured logger (e.g., pino, which Fastify supports natively) with request ID propagation. This will be addressed in Phase 04 (Operations) when observability infrastructure is set up.

overall_summary: The ECP Digital Bank application meets all quality gates for Phase 03 delivery. The server implements all 27 endpoints across 9 modules with full Zod validation, all 10 business rules (RN-01 through RN-10), JWT authentication with bcrypt, and centralized error handling. The web frontend delivers 10 pages with full API integration, 100% design spec compliance (dark theme, lime accent, Inter font), and responsive layout. Test coverage includes 29 test cases across 4 files with acceptance-criteria traceability, covering the 4 highest-risk modules and 6 of 10 business rules. All findings are informational or low severity, with clear recommendations for future iterations. The codebase is well-structured, type-safe, and ready for Phase 04 operations setup.
